title: triticum biunciale
intro: Triticum biunciale, also known as two-grained wheat, is an extinct species of wheat that was once cultivated for its edible grains. The plant is believed to have had medicinal properties, with its grains and leaves potentially being used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Research suggests that Triticum biunciale may have provided health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving digestion, although more studies are needed to confirm these findings. Unfortunately, due to its extinct status, Triticum biunciale is no longer available for herbal preparations, but its historical significance and potential benefits remain of interest to botanists and researchers.
